evidence: packages/predict/sources/pool/pool_accounting.move:44-45 — field doc states the forward-only design: "Aggregate terminal losses that future terminal profits must recover first". pool_accounting.move:245-273 — materialize_expiry_profit: a loss expiry adds initial_loss to net_losses_to_fill (line 260) at terminal-accounting start; profit nets only against ALREADY-accumulated losses (lines 264-266); excess materializes and permanently leaves the netting basis via profit_basis_debits (lines 268-271). There is no mechanism to net a previously-materialized profit against a later loss. packages/predict/sources/pool/plp.move:810-834 — the protocol share (math::mul(profit, config.protocol_reserve_profit_share()), line 819) is split out IMMEDIATELY at each materialization and joined into protocol_reserve_balance (line 823). Reserve is join-only: the only mutations in the entire package are balance::zero() at plp.move:484 and join at plp.move:823 (grep over sources; getter at plp.move:131-133); .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:118-119 (Decision 8) confirms "Currently write-only (no admin withdrawal)". Finding tracked open at OPEN_ISSUES.md:39-43 (Medium) and OPEN_ISSUES.md:110-112 (Decision 5, pending). packages/predict/docs/risks.md does not disclose the netting direction (line 53 only mentions the tunable protocol_reserve_profit_share). Fixture feasibility: tests/helper/flow_test_helpers.move:188-210 (create_expiry, callable twice with different expiries), 429-449 (add_idle_supply_before_expiries — earmark headroom for a second expiry), 298-312 (create_funded_manager_as for bob), 343-368 (prepare_live_oracle_at to re-seed the still-live expiry after the first settlement moves the clock), 465-480 (settle_oracle), 537-557 (redeem_settled), 597-606 (claim_trading_loss_rebate), 665-669 (check_pool asserts protocol_reserve_balance exactly); tests/flows/lifecycle_tests.move:31-90 proves the mint→settle→redeem_settled loop works with exact values; protocol share = 0.4e9 (tests/test_constants.move:68).
scenario: Test A (RED, demonstrates over-take): fx = helpers::setup_market_default(); fx.add_idle_supply_before_expiries(extra) for two-expiry earmark headroom.

let (a_id, a_oracle_id) = fx.create_expiry(test_constants::short_expiry_ms()); let (b_id, b_oracle_id) = fx.create_expiry(test_constants::default_expiry_ms()); — expiry A settles first (200_000 ms), B much later.
let mut alice = fx.create_funded_manager(test_constants::mint_deposit()); let mut bob = fx.create_funded_manager_as(test_constants::bob(), test_constants::mint_deposit());

Take both markets: fx.take_market(a_id, a_oracle_id) gives (pyth, vault, market_a, oracle_a, config); take market_b/oracle_b via fx.scenario_mut().take_shared_by_id::<ExpiryMarket>(b_id) / ::<MarketOracle>(b_oracle_id).

fx.prepare_live_oracle(&config, &mut oracle_a, &mut pyth, default_live_price()); same for oracle_b. Manual two-leg sync (helpers::sync_expiry only handles one active expiry): let mut sync = plp::start_pool_sync(&mut config, &vault); sync.sync_expiry(&mut vault, &mut market_a, &config, &oracle_a, &pyth, fx.clock()); sync.sync_expiry(&mut vault, &mut market_b, &config, &oracle_b, &pyth, fx.clock()); vault.finish_pool_sync(&mut config, sync); — each expiry funded to expiry_cash_floor (sent_to_expiry = 50_000e6).

next_tx(alice): fx.mint(&config, &mut alice, &mut market_a, &oracle_a, &pyth, helpers::min_strike(), constants::pos_inf!(), mint_quantity(), leverage_one_x()) — premium 500e6 + 5e6 fee into A. next_tx(bob): same mint on market_b/oracle_b.
fx.settle_oracle(&config, &mut oracle_a, &mut pyth, 90_000_000_000) — below min_strike, alice's order expires worthless; expiry-A pool profit P_A ~ her premium (+resolved fee residual).
Re-seed B at the post-settlement clock: fx.prepare_live_oracle_at(&config, &mut oracle_b, &mut pyth, default_live_price(), short_expiry_ms() + 2_000).

Two-leg sync again: A takes the settled branch (plp.move:784-808 unregister_settled_expiry -> materialize_expiry_profit) — protocol reserve += 0.4 * chunk1 with net_losses_to_fill == 0. Then fx.claim_trading_loss_rebate(&config, &mut vault, &mut market_a, &oracle_a, &mut alice) returns the residual rebate reserve -> reserve += 0.4 * chunk2. assert_eq!(vault.protocol_reserve_balance(), R_A) where R_A = 0.4 * P_A is hand-derived from premium/fee/rebate-ratio constants.

fx.settle_oracle(&config, &mut oracle_b, &mut pyth, 110_000_000_000) — ITM; fx.redeem_settled(&config, &mut bob, &mut market_b, &oracle_b, &pyth, bob_order, mint_quantity()) pays bob 1e9 from B's cash; expiry-B pool loss L_B ~ 495e6 (payout minus bob's premium/fees).

Single-leg fx.sync_expiry(&mut config, &mut vault, &mut market_b, &oracle_b, &pyth): B's settled branch starts terminal accounting, initial_loss = L_B joins net_losses_to_fill (pool_accounting.move:260), materialized profit = 0, protocol reserve UNCHANGED. fx.claim_trading_loss_rebate(... bob ...) — still no profit, reserve unchanged.

Final assertion (independently derived, order-invariant expectation): assert_eq!(vault.protocol_reserve_balance(), 0.4 * sat_sub(P_A, L_B)). ACTUAL = 0.4 * P_A. Test is RED with discrepancy exactly 0.4 * min(P_A, L_B).

Test B (GREEN mirror, proves order-dependence): identical trades but the LOSS expiry gets short_expiry_ms() and settles first; the later profit then fills net_losses_to_fill (pool_accounting.move:264-266) and the reserve ends at exactly 0.4 * (P_A - L_B). Same economics, different settlement order, different protocol take.
notes: Expected-vs-actual: the existence of net_losses_to_fill shows the design intent is to take the protocol share of NET cross-expiry profit, so the order-independent expected protocol take after both settlements is share * max(0, P_A - L_B) = 0.4 * (P_A - L_B). Actual when the profitable expiry settles first: 0.4 * P_A. The over-take 0.4 * min(P_A, L_B) is borne by LPs and is unrecoverable because the reserve has no outflow path (plp.move:823 join is the only mutation; OPEN_ISSUES Decision 8 confirms write-only). The mirror ordering yields 0.4 * (P_A - L_B), so the protocol take depends purely on settlement order — a genuine accounting defect signature, hence RED/ledger candidate under .redesign/BUGS_FOUND.md discipline. Caveat: OPEN_ISSUES.md Decision 5 (lines 110-112) leaves open "accept the one-directional over-take" as a product outcome; it is NOT yet accepted and NOT disclosed in packages/predict/docs/risks.md, so ACCEPTED-DOCUMENT does not apply today. If Decision 5 later resolves to accept, flip the RED test to a GREEN pin of 0.4 * P_A (the order-dependence pair is still worth keeping as documentation) and add a risks.md disclosure. Test-writing notes: exact P_A/L_B values require pinning the fee-escrow/rebate-reserve residuals (rebate_reserve = f(unresolved_trading_fees_paid), expiry_cash.move:43-49); with zero DEEP stake the rebate_amount is derived from stake_config.rebate_amount(eligible, 0). The strongest minimal variant needing no fee derivation at all: run both orderings and assert the two final protocol_reserve_balance values are EQUAL — fails iff netting is one-directional — then add the exact-value assertions per unit-test rule 1/10.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/pool/plp.move:639-657 (shares_for_supply: total_supply==0 branch mints 1:1, asserts only dusdc_value==0, ignores incentive value); plp.move:387-400 (withdraw burns full supply, claims only pro-rata released; locked remainder + schedule stay in vault); plp.move:553-563 (ENoPlpHolders guards deposit time only; doc claims 'the first future supplier can't capture the whole deposit'); packages/predict/sources/pool/incentive.move:123-134 (claim splits released only), incentive.move:145-164 (compound keeps vesting locked->released on wall-clock regardless of holders), incentive.move:34-36 (doc claims supply+withdraw cannot capture locked remainder); packages/predict/docs/risks.md:91 (claims still-vesting remainder 'stays with remaining holders' — zero-holder window not disclosed); .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:44-47 (finding) and :101-104 (decision 3 'Permanent base PLP supply' still pending). No dead-share/minimum-supply code exists anywhere in plp.move.
scenario: Setup (mirror incentive_tests.move private helpers; no expiry markets so withdraw fee = 0): begin_pool() — plp::init_for_testing + registry::init_for_testing, AdminCap taken, clock at t0 = test_constants::now_ms(); create_default_pyth(&mut scenario, &admin_cap).

LP1 bootstrap: seed pyth via pyth.set_state_for_testing(default_creation_spot, live_ts, live_ts); sync = plp::start_pool_sync(&mut config, &vault); lp1 = vault.supply(&mut config, sync, mint_for_testing<DUSDC>(S, ctx), &pyth, &pyth, &clock, ctx) with S = test_constants::default_initial_supply(). Keep the lp1 coin (unlike incentive_tests::bootstrap_supply, which destroys it).

Incentive deposit at t0: configure_sui_incentive(...) (registry::set_incentive_asset<SUI> bound to the default feed), then registry::deposit_sui_incentive(&registry, &mut vault, &admin_cap, mint_for_testing<SUI>(1_000_000_000, ctx), 86_400_000, &clock). Assert vault.incentive_sui_locked() == 1_000_000_000.

Full LP exit mid-stream at t1 = t0 + 43_200_000 (half the window): clock.set_for_testing(t1); sync = plp::start_pool_sync(...); (dusdc, sui1, deep) = vault.withdraw(&mut config, sync, lp1, &clock, ctx). Assert dusdc.value() == S (fee 0, exact pro-rata), sui1.value() == 500_000_000 (half vested, exact: mul(1e9, div(43_200_000, 86_400_000))), vault.total_supply() == 0, vault.idle_balance() == 0, vault.incentive_sui_locked() == 500_000_000 — the orphaned remainder.

Re-bootstrap: pyth.set_state_for_testing(spot, t1, t1) (fresh for the incentive sync_value gate); sync = plp::start_pool_sync(...); lp2 = vault.supply(&mut config, sync, mint_for_testing<DUSDC>(1_000_000, ctx), &pyth, &pyth, &clock, ctx). Assert lp2.value() == 1_000_000 — 1:1 mint, paying nothing for the 500_000_000 locked SUI (shares_for_supply ignores incentive value in the bootstrap branch).
Capture at stream end t2 = t0 + 86_400_000: clock.set_for_testing(t2); sync = plp::start_pool_sync(...); (dusdc2, sui2, deep2) = vault.withdraw(&mut config, sync, lp2, &clock, ctx).

RED assertion (expected per plp.move:550-551 / incentive.move:34-36 / risks.md:91 — a post-deposit supplier cannot capture the deposit): assert_eq!(sui2.value(), 0). ACTUAL: sui2.value() == 500_000_000 — LP2 paid 1 DUSDC round-trip and captured the entire orphaned half-stream. Tag // KNOWN-FAILING and ledger under .redesign/BUGS_FOUND.md until decision 3 (permanent base supply) lands.
notes: Expected-vs-actual: the package's own docs guarantee a fresh supplier cannot capture deposited incentive (plp.move:550-551 'the first future supplier can't capture the whole deposit'; risks.md:91 'the still-vesting remainder stays with remaining holders'), so the fair expected payout to a re-bootstrap supplier who never held shares during vesting is 0; actual is the full orphaned locked remainder (500_000_000 in the scenario), captured for an arbitrary >=1-unit DUSDC supply. The ENoPlpHolders deposit-time guard does not cover a later full exit, and the bootstrap mint branch checks only dusdc_value == 0, never incentive value. Numbers are exact (no approximation): div(S,S)=1.0 and mul are exact at full-supply withdraw, and 43_200_000/86_400_000 vests exactly half of 1e9. Sibling edge in the same class: any dust/fee left in idle after the full exit instead bricks re-bootstrap with EInvalidInitialSupply (plp.move:648) — also removed by decision 3. Not ACCEPTED-DOCUMENT: decision 3 is still pending (OPEN_ISSUES.md:101-104) and risks.md's disclosure affirmatively claims the opposite of this behavior for the zero-holder case. If the product decision later accepts the behavior, the same test could be flipped to GREEN by pinning sui2.value() == 500_000_000, but as written the discipline calls for RED + ledger.

evidence: expiry_market.move:493-504 (release_settled_pool_cash keeps required_cash = settled_liability + rebate_reserve inside the expiry); expiry_cash.move:43-50 (rebate_reserve from unresolved_trading_fees_paid; required_cash adds it); expiry_cash.move:93-103 (resolve_rebate_reserve_for_fee_basis is the ONLY decrement of the fee basis) with its sole caller expiry_market.move:454-456 (claim_trading_loss_rebate, per-manager); expiry_market.move:898/917/520 (all other outflows preserve the reserve — no sweep/force-resolve anywhere); plp.move:238-241 + 784-808 (settled sync records expiry NAV as 0 via record_expiry_synced(id,0,0) and unregisters) and plp.move:594 (pool value = idle + active_expiry_value → retained reserve invisible to NAV); predict_manager.move:514-536 (resolve_expiry_summary is one-shot, requires open_position_count==0); mitigants: predict_manager.move:142 (PredictManager is shared) + plp.move:288-314 (claim wrapper permissionless, residual returned to idle via receive_expiry_cash) + pool_accounting.move:112-113,156-175 (expiry stays registered after deactivation, so post-sync claims still work). Partial doc disclosure: docs/risks.md:108, docs/overview.md:61, docs/concepts/fees-and-rebates.md:134-149 (invariant + per-claim residual documented; indefinite stranding/no-sweep NOT disclosed).
scenario: let (mut fx, expiry_id, oracle_id, mut manager) = helpers::setup_live_market(test_constants::short_expiry_ms(), test_constants::default_live_price()); fx.scenario_mut().next_tx(test_constants::alice()); let (mut pyth, mut vault, mut market, mut oracle, mut config) = fx.take_market(expiry_id, oracle_id);

Mint one 1x order: let order_id = fx.mint(&config, &mut manager, &mut market, &oracle, &pyth, helpers::min_strike(), constants::pos_inf!(), test_constants::mint_quantity(), test_constants::leverage_one_x()); — trading fee floors at 5_000_000 (independently pinned in lifecycle_tests.move:16); default trading_loss_rebate_rate = 500_000_000 (50%, config_constants.move:296-298) so assert_eq!(market.rebate_reserve(), 2_500_000).

Settle ITM and fully close the position so the manager summary is resolvable and payout liability drains: fx.settle_oracle(&config, &mut oracle, &mut pyth, SETTLEMENT_ITM /* 110e9 */); fx.redeem_settled(&config, &mut manager, &mut market, &oracle, &pyth, order_id, test_constants::mint_quantity()); assert_eq!(market.payout_liability(), 0);

Settled pool sync (the release path under test): let pool_value = fx.sync_expiry_value(&mut config, &mut vault, &mut market, &oracle, &pyth); — hits the is_settled branch (plp.move:238-241): unregister_settled_expiry -> release_settled_pool_cash.

PIN 1 (reserve stays stranded in the settled expiry): helpers::check_market_cash(&market, helpers::expected_market_cash(2_500_000, 0, 2_500_000)); — cash_balance == rebate_reserve == 2_500_000, payout_liability == 0.

PIN 2 (invisible to NAV): assert_eq!(pool_value, vault.idle_balance()); — the settled expiry was recorded at NAV 0, so the 2_500_000 retained inside it is excluded from the pool's DUSDC value (plp.move:594: gross = idle + active_expiry_value).

PIN 3 (no other drain exists — structural): a fresh plp::start_pool_sync/finish_pool_sync no longer visits the deactivated expiry (expected_expiry_markets excludes it), and market cash is unchanged at 2_500_000.

PIN 4 (per-manager claim is the only recovery, including the zero-owed case): let idle_before = vault.idle_balance(); fx.claim_trading_loss_rebate(&config, &mut vault, &mut market, &oracle, &mut manager); — the manager is a net winner (gross_profit = 500_000_000 >= reserve), so eligible_rebate = 0, rebate paid 0, and the FULL residual returns: assert_eq!(market.rebate_reserve(), 0); assert_eq!(market.cash_balance(), 0); assert_eq!(vault.idle_balance(), idle_before + 2_500_000);
notes: Verdict: TESTABLE-NOW / GREEN (pins accepted-but-suboptimal behavior); the open product decision (batch sweep / force-resolve / claim deadline) is not yet in OPEN_ISSUES' decision list, so this should stay an open Medium finding rather than ACCEPTED-DOCUMENT. Why GREEN, not RED: nothing miscomputes and no funds go to a wrong party — the reserve is a genuine trader liability conservatively retained (excluding the contingent residual from NAV is the safe direction under the supply-mark invariant, since the residual size depends on each claimer's future active stake); recovery is permissionless per-manager (PredictManager is shared, the plp wrapper has no owner gate, redeem_settled is permissionless), so a keeper can resolve every manager one-by-one. The real gap is economic liveness/ops: no on-chain enumeration or batch path, and reserve owed to never-claimed managers strands indefinitely while excluded from NAV. The absence-of-API half is not directly assertable; the proposed test pins all observable consequences (reserve survives settled sync inside the expiry, NAV excludes it, the one-shot per-manager claim is the sole drain and returns the full residual for a zero-owed winner). Expected values are independently derived (fee floor 5_000_000 per lifecycle_tests.move:16; default rebate rate 50% per config_constants.move:296-298 -> reserve 2_500_000). Related-but-distinct open items: Low 'permissionless claim griefing' (decision 11, owner gate) and post-compaction escrow semantics (claims still work after compact_storage since materialize_settled_liability is cached and the expiry stays registered). Docs partially disclose the invariant but not the stranding — if the team later accepts no-sweep as final, add one sentence to docs/risks.md and this finding could be re-dispositioned.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/pool/plp.move:383 (withdraw prices against full-NAV dusdc_value from finish_pool_sync); plp.move:388-394 (pro-rata withdraw_amount + fee); plp.move:397 (pays only via expiry_accounting.withdraw_idle); plp.move:594,717-725 (lp_pool_value gross = idle_balance + active_expiry_value); packages/predict/sources/pool/pool_accounting.move:207-210 (withdraw_idle -> assert_withdrawable_idle), 338-343 (aborts EInsufficientActiveAllocationBacking when amount > idle - shortfall, no partial fill), 345-351 (shortfall = active_max_funding_sum - active_net_funding_sum). Disclosure: packages/predict/docs/risks.md:89 and :110 (earmarked backing not LP-withdrawable; such a withdrawal aborts). Pending decision: .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:123 (decision 10). Coverage gap: only register-path test exists at packages/predict/tests/pool/pool_accounting_tests.move:39; no withdraw-path test.
scenario: 1. let mut fx = flow_test_helpers::setup_market_default(); — bootstraps the pool with default_initial_supply = 300_000e6 DUSDC idle (bootstrap PLP coin is hidden in the Fixture, which is why the starving claimant is built as a second LP instead).

2. Second LP supply, keeping the coin: fx.scenario_mut().next_tx(test_constants::admin()); take pyth (take_shared_by_id<PythSource>(fx.pyth_id())), vault (take_shared_by_id<PoolVault>(fx.vault_id())), config (take_shared<ProtocolConfig>()); let sync = plp::start_pool_sync(&mut config, &vault); let lp2 = fx.supply(&mut config, &mut vault, sync, &pyth, 300_000_000_000); return_shared all. Pool: idle 600_000e6, total supply 600_000e6 shares, lp2 = 300_000e6 shares (1:1 price, empty sync is valid pre-expiry).

3. Create TWO expiries (registration earmarks default_max_expiry_funding = 250_000e6 each; cap cannot be raised above this — max_max_expiry_funding == default — so two expiries is the fixture-only way to push the earmark above one LP's backing): let (expiry_a, oracle_a) = fx.create_expiry(test_constants::default_expiry_ms()); let (expiry_b, oracle_b) = fx.create_expiry(test_constants::default_expiry_ms() + 86_400_000). Both registrations pass backing (idle 600_000e6 >= 500_000e6). Two-expiry precedent: tests/flows/expiry_market_gate_tests.move redeem_with_wrong_oracle_aborts.

4. Take both markets/oracles plus pyth/vault/config (take_market for A, plus scenario_mut().take_shared_by_id for market_b/oracle_b); fx.prepare_live_oracle(&config, &mut oracle_a, &mut pyth, test_constants::default_live_price()); same for oracle_b.

5. One full sync + withdraw: let mut sync = plp::start_pool_sync(&mut config, &vault); sync.sync_expiry(&mut vault, &mut market_a, &config, &oracle_a, &pyth, fx.clock()); sync.sync_expiry(&mut vault, &mut market_b, &config, &oracle_b, &pyth, fx.clock()); — rebalance moves the 50_000e6 expiry_cash_floor into each market: idle 500_000e6, active_net_funding_sum 100_000e6, earmark shortfall 400_000e6, NAV still 600_000e6 (exclusion 0: credits+active 100_000e6 <= debits 100_000e6). Then fx.withdraw(&mut config, &mut vault, sync, lp2).

6. Expected abort: #[test, expected_failure(abort_code = pool_accounting::EInsufficientActiveAllocationBacking)] with trailing abort 999 guard. LP2's claim is valid pro-rata (300_000e6 = half of the 600_000e6 NAV; band/fee = 0 with no orders) and the pool genuinely holds 600_000e6 of assets, but free idle is only 100_000e6 (500_000e6 idle - 400_000e6 earmark): the entire withdraw reverts at pool_accounting.move:342 rather than partially filling. Suggested name: withdraw_exceeding_free_idle_aborts_rather_than_partial_filling.

7. Optional companion GREEN assertion in a second test: identical setup, split lp2 down to 50_000e6 shares and withdraw — succeeds with payout 50_000e6, pinning the 'withdrawals are bounded by free idle, smaller exits still work' half of the documented behavior.
notes: Finding still applies verbatim at HEAD: withdraw prices against full NAV (idle + active expiry NAV) but pays only from idle, clamped by the active-allocation earmark, and a large valid pro-rata claim reverts wholesale (EInsufficientActiveAllocationBacking) instead of partially filling. Classified TESTABLE-NOW/GREEN rather than ACCEPTED-DOCUMENT because, although the behavior is explicitly disclosed in docs/risks.md:89/:110 as the cost of the solvency earmark (so it is not a defect — no ledger entry), a pin test adds real information: the withdraw-path abort code is currently covered nowhere (only the register_expiry path at pool_accounting_tests.move:39), and OPEN_ISSUES decision 10 (partial-fill / JIT path vs accept) is still pending — a GREEN pin makes any future silent behavior change (partial fill, earmark relaxation) visible. Construction subtleties verified: (a) the registration-time backing assert (idle >= earmark) guarantees any single later supplier can always exit under ONE expiry, so the starvation needs either the bootstrap LP (whose coin the Fixture hides) or two expiries — the two-expiry route stays entirely on existing helper surface; (b) set_max_expiry_funding cannot manufacture the state since the default cap already equals max_max_expiry_funding (config_constants.move:42-46); (c) funding moves don't change free idle (idle and shortfall fall together), so the numbers are stable across the sync.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/pricing/pricing.move:100-104 (fresh: forward = mul(pyth.spot(), block_scholes_basis()); stale: forward = block_scholes_forward()); pricing.move:88-91 (doc comment declaring the fallback as intended); pricing.move:199-206 (pyth_spot_is_fresh / timestamp_is_fresh — fresh iff now - ts <= max_age_ms, so the switch is a 1 ms clock event); sources/config/config_constants.move:189,200,212 (defaults: pyth 2,000 ms < BS prices 3,000 ms < SVI 60,000 ms — a real stale-Pyth/fresh-BS window exists); sources/strike_exposure/strike_exposure.move:119 (live_inputs feeds the mint/redeem mark, so the jump is externally observable/straddleable); sources/oracle/pyth/pyth_source.move:73-99 (update_from_lazer has no deviation cap vs BS spot, so a diverged fresh Pyth print is production-reachable); docs/risks.md lines 23-37 do NOT disclose the live-mark discontinuity (only staleness-as-mitigation and the settlement fallback); .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:59-60 lists it as accepted Low with no covering decision item.
scenario: let mut fx = oracle_fixture::setup_oracle_default(); // clock = test_constants::now_ms() = 100_000; seeds at live_source_timestamp_ms() = 99_000
let (mut pyth, mut oracle, config) = fx.take_oracle();
fx.prepare_live_oracle(&config, &mut oracle, &mut pyth, test_constants::default_live_price()); // BS spot = forward = 100e9 (basis = 1.0), default SVI, BS freshness ts = 99_000

fx.set_pyth(&mut pyth, 102_000_000_000, test_constants::live_source_timestamp_ms()); // diverged fresh Pyth print +2% (production-reachable: update_from_lazer has no deviation cap); pyth freshness ts = min(99_000, 100_000) = 99_000, same as BS
fx.set_clock_for_testing(101_000); // now - 99_000 = 2_000 == default_pyth_spot_freshness_ms -> Pyth fresh (boundary is inclusive, pricing.move:205)

let quote_fresh = pricing::live_range_probability(config.pricing_config(), &oracle, &pyth, test_constants::default_live_price(), constants::pos_inf!(), fx.clock()); // forward = mul(102e9, 1.0) = 102e9 -> UP tail at strike 100e9 well above ~0.5
fx.set_clock_for_testing(101_001); // +1 ms: Pyth stale (2_001 > 2_000), BS prices still fresh (2_001 <= 3_000), SVI fresh (<= 60_000)

let quote_stale = pricing::live_range_probability(config.pricing_config(), &oracle, &pyth, test_constants::default_live_price(), constants::pos_inf!(), fx.clock()); // forward falls back to block_scholes_forward = 100e9
assert!(quote_fresh > quote_stale); // 1 ms clock advance, zero oracle-data change, mark drops discontinuously (~2% forward jump)
Optionally pin exact values for both quotes (forward 102e9 vs 100e9, same SVI) via the committed tests/helper/reference/generate_pricing_reference.py per unit-tests rule 16
oracle_fixture::return_oracle(pyth, oracle, config); fx.finish();
notes: GREEN, not RED: the fallback is explicitly documented as intended at pricing.move:88-91 and no stated invariant is violated — the discontinuity is the accepted consequence of preferring fresh Pyth spot and falling back to the BS forward. The test pins (a) the inclusive freshness boundary (fresh at exactly now - ts == max_age, stale at +1 ms) and (b) the observable mark jump with zero data change, so any future silent policy change (blended forward, hysteresis, conservative-side quoting) breaks the pin and forces an explicit decision. Two follow-ups worth noting for the orchestrator: (1) docs/risks.md does not disclose this live-mark discontinuity — a one-line disclosure under the oracle section would complement the test; (2) the actual mitigation (hysteresis/blending, or removing the basis-derived forward) is a product decision not currently in OPEN_ISSUES' decisions list (closest is decision 9 on the circuit-breaker envelope). The straddle economics are bounded by the Pyth-vs-BS spot divergence at the boundary times position size, and entry/exit fees still apply, consistent with the Low severity.

evidence: Envelope unchanged at HEAD: packages/predict/sources/config/config_constants.move:375-377 (max_spot_deviation envelope [1, 100_000_000] = 10%/step), :386-388 (max_basis_deviation same), :397-399 and :405-407 (min_basis/max_basis envelope [500_000_000, 2_000_000_000] = [0.5x, 2.0x]); setter path packages/predict/sources/config/market_oracle_config.move:108-135 (assert_basis_bounds_inputs + min_basis < max_basis); AdminCap-gated per-oracle setter packages/predict/sources/oracle/market_oracle.move:336-356; per-push checks have no time-rate limit, only strict source-timestamp monotonicity (market_oracle.move:616-654), so the per-step cap compounds across rapid pushes. Disclosure: packages/predict/docs/risks.md line 25 (operator can steer within the envelope; bounds admin-tunable against a hard envelope so no single call is a no-op), lines 13, 55, 64. Pending decision: .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md 'Decisions to make' item 9. Existing GREEN pins: packages/predict/tests/config/market_oracle_config_tests.move:74-89 (envelope endpoints accepted, including 100_000_000/100_000_000/500_000_000/2_000_000_000) and :109-198 (one-past-envelope aborts for EInvalidMaxSpotDeviation, EInvalidMaxBasisDeviation, EInvalidMinBasis, EInvalidMaxBasis, EInvalidBasisBounds).
notes: The finding still applies (the envelope values at HEAD match the finding exactly: basis up to 2.0x, deviation up to 10%/step), but it is a numeric policy question, not a behavioral defect a unit test can demonstrate. The mechanism the finding asks for (hard envelope on admin setters) already exists and works as designed; the dispute is whether the envelope ceiling values are tight enough. Until OPEN_ISSUES decision 9 picks new cap values, there is no independent 'expected' value to assert (any test would derive expectations from the constants under dispute — circular per unit-tests Rule 1). The envelope edges and all five abort codes are already covered by market_oracle_config_tests.move, so even the GREEN pin adds nothing new. risks.md discloses both the admin-tunability-within-hard-envelope and the operator's ability to steer within the envelope. Residual real gap worth keeping in decision 9: the 10%/step deviation cap has no per-time rate limit (only operator-supplied strictly-increasing ms source timestamps, market_oracle.move:625-629), so a compromised operator can compound steps quickly, and the [0.5,2.0] basis band bounds only forward/spot ratio, not Block-Scholes spot vs Pyth — the 'absolute Pyth tie' item in decision 9. When decision 9 lands with tightened caps, update the endpoint tests to pin the new envelope.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/pool/incentive.move:145-164 — compound's partial-vest branch sets `state.last_compound_ms = now_ms` (line 157) before computing the release; only the balance join is gated by `if (amount > 0)` (lines 161-163), so a zero-rounded release still restarts the vest window. Floor rounding: packages/predict_math/sources/math.move:122-129 (`mul`/`div` both floor). Zero-fraction condition: `div(elapsed, remaining) == 0` when `elapsed * 1e9 < remaining`; max stream = 31_536_000_000 ms (packages/predict/sources/constants.move:56), so elapsed <= 31 ms rounds to 0 on a fresh 1-year stream; dust deposits zero out via `mul(locked, fraction)`. Self-correction: terminal branch incentive.move:151-154 releases the full locked remainder at end_ms (no loss). Drivers: sync_value (incentive.move:108-118, called from plp::supply's value_incentives) and claim (incentive.move:123-134, takes now_ms directly). Observables: plp::incentive_sui_balance / incentive_sui_locked (packages/predict/sources/pool/plp.move:147-153). Fixtures: packages/predict/tests/pool/incentive_tests.move:208-304 (begin_pool, create_default_pyth, bootstrap_supply, configure_sui_incentive, registry::deposit_sui_incentive with caller-chosen duration). Not disclosed in packages/predict/docs/risks.md (line 91 covers in-kind incentive vesting design, not rounding deferral).
notes: GREEN pin, not a ledger candidate: OPEN_ISSUES.md (.claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:63-64) already classifies this Low / "self-correcting, no loss", and verification confirms no invariant violation — full vesting is guaranteed by the terminal branch (incentive.move:151-154), NAV and claim are mutually consistent (both read post-compound released), and the deferral direction is conservative for NAV. Practical reachability: the elapsed<=31ms zero-fraction window on a max-duration stream is finer than on-chain clock granularity (same-checkpoint txs share clock.timestamp_ms() and hit the `now_ms <= last_compound_ms` early-return at incentive.move:147, which does NOT advance the window), so in production the zero-release-with-advance path effectively requires a dust-sized admin deposit (e.g. 1_000 units over 1 day: fraction(1min)=694_444, mul(1000, 694_444)=0 — releases 0 for any sync cadence finer than ~16h of remaining window). Admin controls deposits, so this is a foot-gun, not an attack surface. Suggested home: new tests in packages/predict/tests/pool/incentive_tests.move reusing its private bring-up helpers (add a duration parameter to fund_sui_incentive or call registry::deposit_sui_incentive directly). All expected values above are independent hand-derived floor arithmetic per unit-tests Rule 1. If a future fix is desired, the minimal change is to skip the `last_compound_ms` advance when the computed amount is 0 (or accumulate sub-unit remainders), at which point Test A's assertion flips to 1 and the pin correctly fires.
scenario: Test A (pins zero-release window restart): begin_pool(); create_default_pyth(); bootstrap_supply(); configure_sui_incentive() (existing private helpers in incentive_tests.move:208-286). At clock T0 = test_constants::now_ms(), call registry::deposit_sui_incentive(&registry, &mut vault, &admin_cap, coin::mint_for_testing<SUI>(1_000_000_000, ctx), constants::max_incentive_stream_ms!(), &clock) — 1 SUI vesting over exactly 1 year.

Advance clock.set_for_testing(T0 + 20); refresh the pyth source fresh via pyth.set_state_for_testing(default_creation_spot, live_ts, live_ts); run a follow-on supply: sync = plp::start_pool_sync(&mut config, &vault); vault.supply(&mut config, sync, coin::mint_for_testing<DUSDC>(1_000_000, ctx), &pyth, &pyth, &clock, ctx). This drives incentive::sync_value -> compound(T0+20): div(20, 31_536_000_000) = floor(20e9/31_536_000_000) = 0, release = 0, but last_compound_ms := T0+20.
assert_eq!(plp::incentive_sui_balance(&vault), 0); assert_eq!(plp::incentive_sui_locked(&vault), 1_000_000_000);

Advance clock.set_for_testing(T0 + 40); second follow-on supply -> compound(T0+40): elapsed = 20 again (window was restarted), div(20, 31_535_999_980) = 0, release = 0 again. assert_eq!(plp::incentive_sui_balance(&vault), 0) — pins that two 20ms syncs vest nothing even though 40ms elapsed.

Test B (control deriving the deferral): identical setup, but skip the T0+20 sync; single follow-on supply at T0+40 -> compound(T0+40): div(40, 31_536_000_000) = floor(1.268) = 1; mul(1_000_000_000, 1) = 1. assert_eq!(plp::incentive_sui_balance(&vault), 1) — the 1-unit difference vs Test A is exactly the vesting deferred by the zero-release timestamp advance.

Test C (pins self-correction / no loss): continue Test A; clock.set_for_testing(T0 + constants::max_incentive_stream_ms!()); refresh pyth; one more follow-on supply -> terminal branch (incentive.move:151-154) releases the entire locked remainder. assert_eq!(plp::incentive_sui_balance(&vault), 1_000_000_000); assert_eq!(plp::incentive_sui_locked(&vault), 0).
All expected values are independent hand-derived floor arithmetic (div = x*1e9/y floored, mul = x*y/1e9 floored), not contract output.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/ewma.move:32-39 (new() seeds mean = ctx.gas_price()*float_scaling, variance = 0); packages/predict/sources/ewma.move:71-98 (update(): first-observation branch variance_new = diff_squared when self.variance == 0, lines 90-94; mean moves only by alpha, lines 81-86); packages/predict/sources/ewma.move:45-61 (penalty_fee(): short-circuits on variance==0, else z = div(gas-mean, sqrt(variance)) vs z_score_threshold, lines 51-58); packages/predict/sources/expiry_market.move:372 (ewma::new(ctx) at market creation, seeds mean from creator tx gas); packages/predict/sources/expiry_market.move:661-669 (ewma_penalty folds the trade's gas via update() then reads penalty_fee on EVERY trade — first trader controls tx gas price, hence the seed). Mirrors core verbatim: packages/deepbook/sources/state/ewma.move:74-78 (identical variance==0 ? diff_squared seed). Default-disabled: packages/predict/sources/config/ewma_config.move:47 (enabled: false). Finding listed Low in .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:67-68. Mechanism (not poisonability) disclosed in packages/predict/docs/concepts/fees-and-rebates.md:84 and :91; NOT in packages/predict/docs/risks.md (grep for ewma/congestion/surcharge returns only the unrelated liquidation-gas line).
scenario: Add a new #[test] to packages/predict/tests/ewma_tests.move using the existing helpers (advance_with_gas, config_with, QUANTITY) — pure EwmaState unit test, no protocol fixtures needed.
let config = config_with(config_constants::min_ewma_z_score_threshold!(), true);  // 1-sigma floor, penalty enabled — the most penalty-friendly setting
let mut clock = clock::create_for_testing(test.ctx());
advance_with_gas(&mut test, 1_000, 0);  // market created at gas 1000 -> seed mean = 1000e9, variance = 0
let mut state = ewma::new(test.ctx());

advance_with_gas(&mut test, 100_000, 1_000); clock.set_for_testing(1_000); state.update(&config, &clock, test.ctx());  // ADVERSARY first trade at extreme gas: variance := diff_squared = (100000-1000)^2 -> mean ~= 1990, variance display ~= 9.801e9 (scaled 9.801e18, fits u64), std_dev ~= 99000
advance_with_gas(&mut test, 3_000, 1_000);  // a genuinely high-gas trade that FIRES on the clean 1000->2000->3000 path (z ~= 1.94 sigma)
assert_eq!(state.penalty_fee(&config, QUANTITY, test.ctx()), 0);  // suppressed: z = div((3000-1990)e9, 99000e9) ~= 0.0102 sigma << 1-sigma threshold -> penalty = 0
destroy(state); destroy(config); destroy(clock); end(test);
notes: Finding is real and present at HEAD; nothing has changed it. Expected-vs-actual contrast (independently derivable, no circular logic): the EXISTING penalty_fires_once_z_score_crosses_threshold test (ewma_tests.move:93-115) shows the CLEAN 1000->2000->3000 path at the 1-sigma threshold yields penalty = EXPECTED_PENALTY = 1_000_000; the proposed poisoned path runs the SAME final gas-3000 trade at the SAME 1-sigma threshold and yields 0. Hand-derived poison-path math (math::mul divides by 1e9): mean_new = 0.01*100000 + 0.99*1000 = 1990; diff = 99000, diff_squared(display) = 99000^2 = 9,801,000,000 (scaled 9.801e18 < u64 max 1.8446e19, no overflow); std_dev ~= 99000; z = 1010/99000 ~= 0.0102 sigma <= 1.0 sigma -> 0. So a single attacker-chosen high-gas first trade inflates variance enough to suppress the surcharge for all subsequent traders (variance decays only ~1%/update at default alpha=0.01, so it persists for hundreds of updates). Classified TESTABLE-NOW + GREEN, not RED: (1) the module deliberately mirrors DeepBook core's gas-price EWMA (same variance==0 ? diff_squared seed at deepbook/.../ewma.move:74-78) — the contract behaves exactly as the mirrored design dictates; (2) no protocol invariant states the surcharge MUST fire under congestion, so a RED test asserting "penalty should fire after poisoning" would be inventing an unspecified spec (violates unit-tests Rule 1/15 discipline); (3) the penalty is disabled by default and the surcharge is a non-solvency LP-compensation add-on that rides into expiry cash as surplus (fees-and-rebates.md:93); (4) Low severity in OPEN_ISSUES. The right test PINS the accepted-but-suboptimal behavior (penalty suppressed) so any future hardening (e.g. clamping the initial variance seed or gating on a minimum observation count) is regression-detectable. Alternative classification ACCEPTED-DOCUMENT is defensible (default-disabled + mirrors-core + Low), but a clean pin test is trivially constructible on the existing ewma test surface and adds regression value, so TESTABLE-NOW/GREEN is the more precise call. Note: poisonability itself is NOT disclosed in user-facing risks.md (only the mechanism is described in fees-and-rebates.md) — if this were upgraded from GREEN-pin to a remediation, a risks.md disclosure or a variance-seed clamp would be the fix; both are out of scope for this read-only triage.

evidence: strike_nav_matrix.move:41 (strike_quantity: u64); strike_nav_matrix.move:215 (weighted_quantity(qty, math::mul(qty, strike))); packages/predict_math/sources/math.move:122-124 (mul: u128 product cast `as u64`, cast-abort above u64::MAX); strike_nav_matrix.move:356-365 (apply_weighted_delta native u64 add at :359 — insert-path accumulator, no named code); strike_nav_matrix.move:286-291 (live_value cross-page page_totals fold, same native add — valuation-path abort); packages/predict/sources/order.move:126-129 (per-order cap u32 lots × 10_000 = 4.295e13 raw = $42.95M; bounds one order, not aggregate); packages/predict/sources/expiry_market.move:381-409 (pool_nav hot path) and strike_exposure.move:106-137 (valuation_components → live_value); packages/predict/sources/constants.move:29 (DUSDC 6 decimals); not disclosed in packages/predict/docs/risks.md (no overflow/capacity entry). Threshold: Σ qty·strike/1e9 > u64::MAX = 1.8447e19 → at BTC strike 100_000e9 (multiplier 1e5) Σqty > 1.8447e14 raw = $184.5M aggregate quantity; at the BTC fixture's grid-top $150k strike, $123M — i.e. 3 max-size ($42.95M) orders (3 × 6.442e18 = 1.93e19 > u64::MAX). Single-order mul cast abort needs strike > ~4.295e14 ($429k). Low-priced assets unreachable ($4 asset → ~$4.6T threshold).
scenario: Add a test to packages/predict/tests/strike_exposure/index/strike_nav_matrix_tests.move (same-package #[test_only] module; public(package) surface already used there). Reuse its constants BTC_SPOT = 100_000_000_000_000 and TICK_SIZE = 1_000_000_000.

Test 1 (insert-path accumulator overflow): let ctx = &mut tx_context::dummy(); let grid = strike_grid::new_centered(BTC_SPOT, TICK_SIZE); let mut nav = strike_nav_matrix::new(&grid, 0, ctx); — grid spans [$50k, $150k], 100_001 strikes.
const MAX_ORDER_QTY: u64 = 42_949_672_950_000; // u32::MAX lots x position_lot_size!() = largest production-mintable order (order.move:129), $42.95M DUSDC
let lower = grid.max_strike() - 2 * TICK_SIZE; let higher = lower + TICK_SIZE; // strike ~1.5e14, per-insert strike_quantity delta = mul(MAX_ORDER_QTY, lower) ~ 6.442e18
nav.insert_range(&grid, lower, higher, MAX_ORDER_QTY, 0); twice — running slot total 1.288e19, still fits.

Third nav.insert_range(&grid, lower, higher, MAX_ORDER_QTY, 0); — expected (correct behavior): succeeds, since no protocol rule caps aggregate open interest at a strike (mint flow enforces only the per-order u32-lot cap and pool backing, which deep-ITM mints barely consume); then assert live_value over a 2-point curve equals the hand-derived Sum(qty x up_price) and drain with remove_range x3. Actual: native u64 add overflow inside apply_weighted_delta (strike_nav_matrix.move:359) — unnamed VM arithmetic abort. Per rule 15, do NOT encode as expected_failure of the arithmetic abort; leave RED, tag KNOWN-FAILING, ledger in .redesign/BUGS_FOUND.md.

Test 2 (valuation-path brick — mints succeed, NAV aborts): insert MAX_ORDER_QTY at three lower boundaries in three different 128-tick pages near the top of the grid, e.g. l_i = grid.max_strike() - (600 - 200*i) * TICK_SIZE for i in 0..3 (pages 776/778/779; each page total ~6.44e18 < u64::MAX so all inserts succeed). Build a flat curve via let svi = market_oracle::new_svi_params(a, b, rho, m, sigma) with wing-flat params (mirror pricing_guard_tests.move:56 usage; pick params so compute_up_price rounds equal at these deep strikes — find_gap (pricing.move:286-311) then never subdivides and build_curve(svi, BTC_SPOT, TICK_SIZE, l0, l2 + TICK_SIZE) returns exactly 2 points => one segment spanning all 3 pages). Call nav.live_value(&grid, &curve, l0, l2 + TICK_SIZE, 0) and assert the hand-derived total. Actual: accumulate_segment_values (strike_nav_matrix.move:286-291) folds 3 x 6.44e18 = 1.93e19 via apply_weighted_delta:359 — native abort. This is the 'bricked valuation' shape: pool_nav / supply / withdraw sync abort while every mint succeeded.
notes: Confirmed real defect at HEAD, not an accepted/documented tradeoff (risks.md has no overflow/capacity disclosure). Severity 'Low' is defensible but the consequence is sharp: the abort is a native arithmetic error with no named code, on the pool NAV hot path (pool_nav -> valuation_components -> live_value), and risks.md:87 notes every supply/withdraw must value every active market — so one over-threshold BTC expiry bricks LP supply/withdraw protocol-wide until enough positions are redeemed (live redeem itself survives: it prices per-order ranges, not live_value). Realism: $123-184M aggregate minted quantity in one BTC expiry is whale-scale but reachable — per-order cap is $42.95M, deep-ITM mints need almost no LP backing so max_expiry_funding does not bound aggregate quantity, and only high-priced (BTC-class) underlyings are exposed (multiplier = strike/1e9). The same u64-accumulator pattern likely also affects strike_payout_tree terms (out of scope for this finding key, worth a follow-up triage). Fix direction (for later, not this pass): widen strike_quantity accumulators to u128 (it is only consumed via div to recover strike_avg) or store tick-index-weighted quantity instead of raw-strike-weighted (multiplier becomes tick index <= 100_000 of qty in lots, removing ~1e5 price scaling).

evidence: packages/predict/sources/pool/plp.move:288-314 (public `claim_trading_loss_rebate` wrapper: gates only version, `assert_not_valuation_in_progress`, `assert_market_oracle`, `assert_registered_expiry` — NO `manager.assert_owner(ctx)`); packages/predict/sources/expiry_market.move:439-490 (inner package fn: `manager.update_stake(ctx)` at :464, `config.stake_config().rebate_amount(eligible_rebate, manager.active_stake())` at :465-467, residual reserve paid back to pool at :477-478, no owner gate; early-return zero when nothing owed at :450-452); packages/predict/sources/config/stake_config.move:49-55 + :78-92 (`rebate_amount = mul(eligible_rebate, benefit_ratio(active_stake))`, `benefit_ratio` is monotonic non-decreasing in active_stake, `benefit_ratio(0)=0`); packages/predict/sources/predict_manager.move:540-545 (`update_stake` is a same-epoch no-op via `if (self.stake_epoch == ctx.epoch()) return`), :514-536 (`resolve_expiry_summary` REMOVES the per-expiry summary → claim is one-shot), :418-426 (`deposit_permissionless` credits payout with no owner check); packages/predict/docs/risks.md:76,92,108 (discloses permissionless *liquidation* and free DEEP unstake, but NOT permissionless rebate claim / stake-timing grief — so this is undisclosed); .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:71-72 (finding) and :125-126 (decision 11 still OPEN: "Gate the public wrapper, or accept permissionless resolution").
scenario: Setup: `let mut f = flow_test_helpers::setup_market_default();` then `f.create_expiry(expiry_ms)` to get (oracle_id, market_id); take the PoolVault by `f.vault_id()`. Create a victim manager owned by alice: `let mut mgr = f.create_funded_manager(deposit);` (sender now alice).

Generate a trading fee + a losing position: as alice, `f.prepare_live_oracle(...)` then `let oid = f.mint(config, &mut mgr, &mut market, &oracle, &pyth, lower, higher, qty, leverage);` (mint records `trading_fees_paid > 0` on the manager's expiry summary).

Settle the market worthless for that order's range: `f.settle_oracle(config, &mut oracle, &mut pyth, settlement_price)` with a price outside the order range so terminal payout = 0; then `f.redeem_settled(config, &mut mgr, &mut market, &oracle, &pyth, oid, qty)` — closes the position (open_position_count -> 0) with `gross_received = 0`, so later `gross_profit = 0` and `eligible_rebate = rebate_reserve_for_fee_basis(trading_fees_paid) > 0`.

ATTACK leg — griefer front-runs at zero active stake: do NOT stake/activate for alice. `f.scenario_mut().next_tx(bob);` (bob = non-owner griefer). Call `f.claim_trading_loss_rebate(config, &mut vault, &mut market, &oracle, &mut mgr);` — the wrapper has no owner gate so bob succeeds; inside, `update_stake` is a same-epoch no-op, `active_stake = 0`, `benefit_ratio(0) = 0`, `rebate_amount = mul(eligible_rebate, 0) = 0`.

Assert the defect: victim manager DUSDC balance is UNCHANGED (rebate credited = 0), the residual reserve was diverted to the pool (assert PoolVault idle increased by ~`eligible_rebate`, proving the value left the victim and did NOT go to bob), and a subsequent alice re-claim is a no-op (summary already consumed -> `resolve_expiry_summary` returns (0,0) -> `balance::zero`).

CONTROL leg (separate test, identical setup): stake DEEP for alice via `vault.stake_deep(&mut mgr, deep_coin, ctx)` with `active_stake >= upper_benefit_power`, `f.scenario_mut().next_epoch(alice)` so the stake activates (benefit_ratio = 1e9), then alice claims -> victim manager DUSDC increases by exactly `eligible_rebate`. The delta between control (`eligible_rebate`) and attack (`0`) is the griefing loss.
notes: Finding confirmed at HEAD: both wrappers are permissionless. `plp.move` public `claim_trading_loss_rebate` lacks `manager.assert_owner(ctx)` (contrast the adjacent `stake_deep`/`unstake_deep` at plp.move:417-449 which DO call `manager.assert_owner`), and the inner `expiry_market.move` fn has no owner gate and credits via `deposit_permissionless`. The rebate is stake-scaled (`rebate_amount = eligible_rebate * benefit_ratio(active_stake)`, monotonic, `benefit_ratio(0)=0`) and one-shot (`resolve_expiry_summary` removes the summary; re-claim returns zero). So a non-owner can permanently resolve the victim's rebate at whatever `active_stake` exists at call time. update_stake is a same-epoch no-op, so a griefer front-running before the victim's stake activates locks in a strictly-lower rebate. EXPECTED vs ACTUAL: with an owner gate (decision-11 fix) the owner controls timing and claims after activation -> receives `eligible_rebate` (up to full benefit_ratio). At HEAD a griefer claims while `active_stake=0` -> victim credited `0`, the entire reserve residual is diverted to the LP pool (not to the griefer — pure griefing), and the summary is consumed so the victim cannot recover it. Net victim loss = `eligible_rebate`. Classified RED because (a) it is a real, demonstrable value loss to the victim, (b) it is NOT disclosed in risks.md, and (c) decision 11 is still OPEN (not formally accepted). Caveat: if the team resolves decision 11 to "accept permissionless resolution" and documents it in risks.md, this should be re-tagged GREEN/ACCEPTED-DOCUMENT — the same test then pins accepted behavior. The attacker gains nothing (value goes to the pool), so severity is correctly "Low". Note the fixture's `claim_trading_loss_rebate` helper already uses the bare scenario ctx (no proof), so swapping the sender via `next_tx(bob)` is sufficient to exercise the missing owner gate with existing helpers — no new source seam needed.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/oracle/pyth/pyth_source.move:115-123 (value_in_dusdc, unchecked `as u64` downcast at 122; line 121 comment documents the VM abort as the intentional out-of-domain backstop); pyth_source.move:164-166 (pow10 via 10u128.pow — Arm A abort at decimals>=36) and 168-170 (ceil_div); packages/predict/sources/constants.move:24,29 (float_scaling_decimals=9, dusdc_decimals=6 → exponent = decimals+3); packages/predict/sources/pool/incentive.move:117 (sole production call site, sync_value) and 123-134 (claim is in-kind, oracle-free — finding's "claim paths bricked" clause is wrong at HEAD); packages/predict/sources/pool/plp.move:68,72 (incentives concretely SUI/DEEP), 337+701-715 (only supply values incentives), 397-400 (withdraw pays in-kind, never calls value_in_dusdc); packages/predict/sources/registry.move:284,301-326 (decimals from canonical Currency<T>; only SUI/DEEP deposit paths → high-decimal arm unreachable in production); packages/predict/docs/risks.md:91 (in-kind payout needs no oracle, exits can't be blocked); fixtures: packages/predict/tests/helper/oracle_fixture.move:59-145, pyth_source.move:244-253 (set_state_for_testing), packages/predict/tests/pool/incentive_tests.move:152-178 (flow template)
scenario: Add tests (e.g. tests/oracle/pyth_source_value_tests.move); value_in_dusdc is public(package) so test modules in the package can call it directly.

GREEN boundary-success pin (envelope must not shrink): `let mut fx = oracle_fixture::setup_oracle_default(); let (mut pyth, oracle, config) = fx.take_oracle(); pyth.set_state_for_testing(1_000 * 1_000_000_000 /* SUI=$1,000 → spot 1e12 */, test_constants::live_source_timestamp_ms(), test_constants::live_source_timestamp_ms());` then `assert_eq!(pyth.value_in_dusdc(std::u64::max_value!(), 9), std::u64::max_value!());` — hand-derived: ceil((2^64-1)*1e12/1e12) = 2^64-1 exactly. Clean up via oracle_fixture::return_oracle + fx.finish().

GREEN Arm-B downcast abort: same setup with spot = 2_000 * 1_000_000_000 (=2e12, SUI at $2,000); `#[test, expected_failure(arithmetic_error, location = deepbook_predict::pyth_source)]`; call `pyth.value_in_dusdc(std::u64::max_value!(), 9);` → result 2*(2^64-1) overflows the `as u64` downcast; end with `abort 999` guard.

GREEN Arm-A pow10 abort: `#[test, expected_failure(arithmetic_error, location = deepbook_predict::pyth_source)]`; `pyth.value_in_dusdc(1, 36);` → exponent 39, 10u128.pow(39) > u128::MAX aborts; `abort 999` guard. Note in a comment this arm is production-unreachable at HEAD (incentives fixed to SUI d=9 / DEEP d=6 from Currency metadata).

Optional GREEN flow-level pair (mirrors incentive_tests.move:152-178 template, reusing begin_pool/create_default_pyth/bootstrap_supply/configure_sui_incentive/fund_sui_incentive with a 1e19-MIST mint_for_testing deposit): advance clock past the stream end so the deposit is fully released, set spot = 2e12 with fresh timestamps, assert plp::supply aborts (arithmetic_error in pyth_source) while plp::withdraw on the bootstrap holder still succeeds — pins that an out-of-range incentive feed bricks new supply only, never LP exit (extends risks.md:91).
notes: Finding still applies mechanically but two of its claims are wrong at HEAD. Derived envelope: exponent = asset_decimals + 3; Arm A (pow10 u128 overflow) aborts iff decimals >= 36 — unreachable in production because incentive state is concretely IncentiveState<SUI>(d=9)/IncentiveState<DEEP>(d=6) with decimals read from canonical Currency<T> metadata and no generic deposit path exists; Arm B (u64 downcast) aborts iff released incentive value > u64::MAX micro-DUSDC ≈ $18.4 trillion (for SUI: amount*spot > 1.845e31, e.g. the entire 10B-SUI supply deposited at >$1,845/SUI) — economically out of domain. The intermediate u128 product can never overflow and ceil_div's internal add is unreachable for d <= 9. The "claim paths bricked" clause is false: claim/withdraw are oracle-free in-kind splits; only the supply-side NAV valuation can abort, so LP exit is never blocked (already disclosed in docs/risks.md:91). The abort itself is accepted in-code ("VM abort is the out-of-domain backstop", pyth_source.move:121) and matches the repo rule against adding overflow asserts to replace VM aborts, so tests are GREEN pins of the envelope (the boundary-success test is the informative one — it catches a future regression that shrinks the domain, e.g. narrowing the u128 intermediate). Re-triage if a generic incentive-asset deposit path is ever added (set_incentive_asset<T> at registry.move:275 is already generic): Arm A becomes reachable for a >=36-decimal coin and the verdict flips toward RED. Related open product decision #7 in OPEN_ISSUES.md (ceil vs round-down) is orthogonal to overflow.

evidence: Finding: .claude/predict-design/OPEN_ISSUES.md:75-77. Native u64 multiply still at HEAD: packages/predict/sources/ewma.move:35 (`new`, market creation), :53 (`penalty_fee`), :84 (`update`) — all `ctx.gas_price() * math::float_scaling!()`. Hot trade path confirmed: packages/predict/sources/expiry_market.move:661-669 (`ewma_penalty` calls update then penalty_fee), invoked from mint at :714 and live redeem at :780. Bound verified exact: float_scaling = 1e9 (packages/predict_math/sources/math.move:119); u64::MAX/1e9 = 18_446_744_073, and 18_446_744_073*1e9 fits u64 while 18_446_744_074*1e9 overflows — matching the inline comments at ewma.move:34, :52, :83 ("Gas price must exceed 18_446_744_073 MIST to overflow scaling; realistic Sui gas is far lower, and the VM abort is the backstop"). Secondary path also checked: variance fold at ewma.move:88-89 (`math::mul(diff, diff)`, u128 intermediate per math.move:122-124) overflows the u64 downcast only when the gas deviation exceeds ~135_818 MIST; Sui's protocol-enforced max tx gas price (100_000 MIST) keeps both bounds unreachable (~184_467x margin on the headline bound), so neither is attacker-controllable.
notes: The finding's ask ("verify the bound math") is complete and the bound is correct as documented. Status: the native multiply still exists, but every multiply site carries the verified bound comment naming the VM abort as the intentional backstop, which matches repo policy (.claude/rules/move.md: do not add explicit overflow asserts solely to replace Move's primitive VM aborts). Not disclosed in packages/predict/docs/risks.md (no ewma/gas-overflow entry; only an unrelated liquidation-gas note at risks.md:119) — disclosure lives in the source comments, which is appropriate for a non-reachable arithmetic bound rather than a user-facing risk. Testability note: a boundary test IS technically constructible with existing fixtures (ewma_tests.move:21-25 shows `test.ctx_builder().set_gas_price(...)`; assert penalty math works at gas = 18_446_744_073, and `#[test, expected_failure(arithmetic_error, ...)]` at 18_446_744_074), but it adds no information: it would pin a compile-time arithmetic constant (u64::MAX / float_scaling) against a gas price ~184,467x above Sui's protocol max_gas_price cap (100_000 MIST), i.e. a production-unreachable state, conflicting with unit-tests.md Rule 12 (production-valid fixtures). The only way the bound moves is if the 1e9 scaling changes, which would invalidate the documented constant in the same edit. Recommend closing the OPEN_ISSUES bullet as verified-and-documented.

evidence: packages/predict/sources/oracle/market_oracle.move:411-418 (assert_valid_svi: only |rho|<=1.0 via EInvalidSviRho and sigma in [svi_sigma_min, svi_sigma_max] via EInvalidSviSigma); :318 (called from update_svi); :48-49 (a,b are u64 so a>=0,b>=0 only by type); packages/predict/sources/constants.move:106,109 (sigma bounds 1e-3..100.0; no svi_b_* constants remain); git 3c7917f9 (original fix added b in [1e-4,1.0] EInvalidSviB) vs git b10a37d7 = HEAD (b bound deliberately removed); packages/predict/tests/oracle/market_oracle_tests.move:51-60 (existing GREEN pin: b unbounded, accepts 0 and pos_inf); packages/predict/sources/pricing/pricing.move:172-177 (range_price clamps inverted UP prices to 0 instead of aborting — 3c7917f9 replaced ERangePriceUnderflow with this clamp; reused in find_gap ~:300-303); pricing.move:233-267 (compute_nd2: w(k)=a+b*inner with read-time ECannotBeNegative :251 and EZeroVariance :257); packages/predict/docs/risks.md:25 (SVI surface is the trusted operator's to set within hygiene bounds); tests/helper/oracle_fixture.move:181-194 (prepare_real_oracle pushes arbitrary SVI through production update_svi); tests/pricing/pricing_exact_tests.move:51,65 (existing pattern calling live_range_probability)
scenario: let mut fx = oracle_fixture::setup_oracle_default(); (tests/helper/oracle_fixture.move:121; creation spot 50_100*1e9, tick 1e9)
let (mut pyth, mut oracle, config) = fx.take_oracle(); (oracle_fixture.move:131)

Build a butterfly-arbitrageable SVI that passes the validation box: let svi = market_oracle::new_svi_params(0, 1_000_000 * float!(), i64::from_parts(float!(), true), i64::zero(), constants::svi_sigma_min!()); — a=0, b=1e6 (allowed: b is unbounded at HEAD), rho=-1.0 (inclusive boundary accepted, pinned at market_oracle_tests.move:70-74), m=0, sigma=1e-3. For k>0 this gives w(k) ~= b*sigma^2/(2k), making the UP curve N(-(k+w/2)/sqrt(w)) INCREASING in strike for k in (0, ~0.29) — textbook butterfly arbitrage.

fx.prepare_real_oracle(&config, &mut oracle, &mut pyth, spot, spot, svi); (oracle_fixture.move:181) — routes through production update_svi; this call SUCCEEDING is pin #1: update_svi performs no no-arbitrage/monotonicity validation.
Pick grid-valid strikes in the inversion band: K1 = 55_500*1e9 (k=ln(K1/spot)~=0.102), K2 = 61_200*1e9 (k~=0.200), both on the 1e9 tick grid.

Pin the inversion via tail quotes: up1 = pricing::live_range_probability(cfg, &oracle, &pyth, K1, constants::pos_inf!(), fx.clock()); up2 = same with K2; assert_eq! both against scipy-derived exact values (committed reference generator per unit-tests Rule 16; approx up1~=0.124, up2~=0.180, i.e. up2 > up1 — non-monotone digital prices). Pin #2.

Pin the clamp: assert_eq!(pricing::live_range_probability(cfg, &oracle, &pyth, K1, K2, fx.clock()), 0); — the inverted range (positive true probability) quotes exactly 0 instead of aborting or going negative (pricing.move:172-177). Pin #3.
oracle_fixture::return_oracle(pyth, oracle, config); fx.finish();
notes: VERDICT on the VERIFY question: the SVI-validation fix (3c7917f9) did NOT cover no-arbitrage/monotonicity — it added only a parameter box, and even that shrank at HEAD. Exactly what IS validated by assert_valid_svi: (1) |rho| <= 1.0 inclusive; (2) sigma in [1e-3, 100.0] inclusive. Implicit by u64 type only: a >= 0, b >= 0 — which makes Gatheral's total-variance non-negativity a + b*sigma*sqrt(1-rho^2) >= 0 VACUOUSLY satisfied (that condition exists to handle negative a, unrepresentable here; w(k) >= a >= 0 for all k when |rho| <= 1). What is NOT validated: (a) no bound on b AT ALL — the original fix's b in [1e-4, 1.0] (EInvalidSviB) was deliberately removed in b10a37d7 ('align simulations with latest contracts', real Block Scholes data violated it) and an existing GREEN test pins b-unbounded; (b) no bound on a (u64) or m (i64 magnitude); (c) no butterfly no-arbitrage condition (Gatheral-Jacquier g(k) >= 0); (d) no wing-slope/Lee moment bound; (e) no UP-price monotonicity check at write or read — the read side intentionally CLAMPS inverted ranges to 0 (range_price, pricing.move:172-177; same commit replaced the ERangePriceUnderflow abort); (f) calendar arbitrage is structurally N/A (each MarketOracle stores one SVI slice for one expiry; nothing cross-expiry exists on-chain to compare). Degenerate-but-accepted corner: assert_valid_svi accepts a=0,b=0, which then aborts every live quote with EZeroVariance (pricing.move:257) until the next SVI push — operator-failure scope. Why GREEN not RED: the clamp is deliberate, code-commented behavior; an arbitrageable surface can only be pushed by the authorized MarketOracleCap operator, whose freedom to shape the surface within hygiene bounds is explicitly disclosed in docs/risks.md:25 ('within those bounds the surface is the operator's to set'); a full on-chain g(k)>=0 check over a continuum is impractical. The proposed test pins three facts: update_svi accepts an arbitrageable surface, digital prices invert, and the inverted range quotes 0 (not negative, no abort). Partial coverage already exists (market_oracle_tests.move:20-74 pins the rho/sigma box and b-unbounded), but nothing currently exercises the end-to-end consequence through prepare_real_oracle, so the scenario test adds information. Recommend also updating OPEN_ISSUES.md to flip this entry from 'verify' to a documented accepted tradeoff (optionally re-introducing a sane wing-slope cap, e.g. on b*(1+|rho|), as a future product decision now that b is fully unbounded). If the team decides update_svi SHOULD reject arbitrageable surfaces, this same test flips to the RED spec test.
